sections.paragraph:
εὐπορέω (s. next entry, also εὐπορος ‘well off’) aor. 3 sg. εὐπόρησεν Wsd 10:10; impf. mid. 3 sg. εὐπορεῖτο (ηὐ-v.l.; on the augm. s. W-S. §12, 5b; B-D-F §67, 1); aor. pass. subj. 3 sg. εὐπορηθῇ; ptc. εὐπορηθείς LXX; to prosper financially, have plenty, be well off (the mid. is used now and then in this sense, which usu. belongs to the active [TestJob 12:1; 24:4]: e.g. the mid. is found Aristot., Oec. 2, 23; Polyb. 1, 66, 5; 5, 43, 8; Jos., Ant. 17, 214, Vi. 28; cp. Lev 25:26, 49) abs. (as Theopomp.: 115 Fgm. 36 Jac. [in Athen. 6, 275c]; Lucian, Bis Acc. 27; SIG 495, 66; PGM 4, 3125; Just., A I, 67, 6) καθὼς εὐπορεῖτό τις according to each one’s (financial) ability Ac 11:29.—DELG s.v. πόρος. M-M. TW. Spicq.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. sg. = singular

impf. impf. = imperfect

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

augm. augm. = augment

W-S. W-S. = GWiner, Grammatik des neutestamentlichen Sprachidioms, ed. PSchmiedel—List 6

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

subj. subj. = subjunctive; subject

ptc. ptc. = participle

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

usu. usu. = usually

TestJob TestJob = Testament of Job—List 2

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

abs. abs. = absolute

Theopomp Theopomp , hist., IV b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

Athen Athen , III a.d.—List 5

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 410
